name: Basic Vegetable Soup
recipeInstructions:
Use about 6 cups liquid. You may mix chicken and beef broth (canned lowfat, not bouillon cubes) with water and tomato juice or vegetable juice cocktail to suit your tastes.
1. Melt 4 tablespoons margarine in a skillet. Add the chopped green peppers, carrots, celery, and onion. Saute on low heat until the vegetables are softened and the onion is lightly browned. Add this to the broth mixture which has been placed in a large, heavy pot with lid.
2. Add to this the potatoes, green beans, lima beans, and corn. Cover and simmer for about 45 minutes or until all the vegetables are done. Add the salt and pepper.
3. Remove about 2 cups of the mixture and put into a blender. Puree this mixture and return it to the pot with the rest of the soup. Turn off heat and cover the soup. Best if allowed to set for up to 1 hour to blend flavors.
Since this is a basic recipe for soup, you can add your preferred vegetables and follow the directions above to finish the soup. Some suggestions include adding a bay leaf, shredded cabbage, chopped zucchini, diced turnips, fresh tomatoes, okra, or parsley. For added nutrition, serve with a sprinkling of lowfat cheese on top of the hot soup. Also good sprinkled with toasted croutons. This stores very well in the refrigerator and is actually improved with time.
